tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Forty Strangers Transform a Family's Home in One Beautiful Weekend
stories.summary: A community of strangers rallied to renovate a struggling Trafford family's home in 48 hours, turning despair into dignity and hope.

stories.headline: A Mother's Love: 500 Toys Delivered to Sick Children in Her Son's Memory
stories.summary: Naheed Akhtar honors her son Bobby, who died of cancer at two, by delivering over 500 toys to hospitalized children alongside Team Bobby.

stories.headline: Scientists Race Against Time to Save Species From Extinction—and Succeed
stories.summary: Scientists rescued seeds from Chile's last wild Dendroselis neriifolia tree; seven seedlings now offer hope at Kew Gardens.

stories.headline: Senior's Clutch Hit Honors Late Uncle, Brings First Sectional Title Home
stories.summary: Senior Dane Chock's pinch-hit brings Crystal Lake South their first sectional title since 2022, honoring his late uncle Ray.

stories.headline: Solar Panels That Turn Seawater Into Drinking Water—No Toxic Waste
stories.summary: Chunlei Guo's laser-etched aluminum panels turn real seawater into drinking water while harvesting salt, leaving no toxic brine.

stories.headline: Brothers' Lemonade Stand Raises $23,000+ for Veterans and Military Families
stories.summary: Brothers Noah and Cole Dingels' fifth annual lemonade stand raised $23,000 for veterans and military families at Olympic Hills.

stories.headline: 19-Year-Old Champion and Cinderella Qualifier Share a Beautiful Moment
stories.summary: Mirra Andreeva, 19, wins her first Grand Slam at the French Open, while qualifier Maja Chwalińska—only the second ever to reach a Grand Slam final—inspires as an underdog.

stories.headline: Police Called on Lemonade Stand Become Customers and Community Cheerleaders
stories.summary: Officers responded to a complaint about brothers Parez and Jakkhi's lemonade stand, then became cheerleaders helping them raise $280 for their dreams.

stories.headline: Rare Frog Rebounds: 16,000 Dusky Gophers Now Thriving in Mississippi Wetlands
stories.summary: Memphis Zoo scientists celebrate releasing nearly 500 dusky gopher frogs, bringing the species population to over 16,000 in Mississippi wetlands since 2017.

stories.headline: Mangrove Forests Stop Vanishing—and Start Growing Back Around the World
stories.summary: Tulane University researchers discovered mangrove forests globally are no longer declining—gains have outpaced losses for 16 years, marking a turning point for conservation.

stories.headline: India's Elusive Caracal Spotted Again After Decades—Ecosystem Thrives
stories.summary: India's elusive caracal—the 'flying cat'—photographed at Kuno National Park for the first time in decades, signaling thriving ecosystem recovery.
